title: A leader of the 2014 U.S. Ebola response compares then to now
summary: As the world deals with an outbreak of Ebola that has no known cure, Susan Reichle, who last year co-founded Aid Transition Alliance to support former USAID professionals, discusses the…

title: STAT+: Trump’s health care affordability czar touts Medicaid cuts to hospital leaders
summary: Hospital execs say they're working to mitigate the impacts of the looming cuts.

title: Opinion: Why STAT is sticking with ‘health care’ as two words
summary: Is it “health care” or “healthcare”? STAT readers had strong feelings.

title: STAT+: Wearables, and the flood of data they generate, inch closer to entering the clinic
summary: Wearables generate a lot of health data, but it exists outside the clinic for the most part. New moves from Oura and Whoop may change that.

title: Opinion: Ending animal testing could set back xenotransplantation just as the field is poised for a breakthrough
summary: “Innovations like xenotransplantation would never have reached clinical trials without animal experimentation,” writes transplant surgeon Joshua Mezrich.

title: STAT+: GSK to buy Nuvalent, maker of targeted cancer drugs, for $10.6B
summary: GSK is buying Nuvalent, the maker of targeted cancer drugs, for $10.6 billion, a sizable addition to the latest run of biopharma acquisitions.

title: Alcohol study discarded by Trump officials is published in scientific journal
summary: A government-commissioned study had been meant to inform new dietary guidelines, and found risks associated with even light drinking.

title: Health workers at the epicenter of Congo’s Ebola outbreak labor with little pay or rest
summary: Health workers at the epicenter of Congo's Ebola outbreak are laboring with little pay or rest.

title: STAT+: AstraZeneca’s GLP-1 pill shows promise in obesity, diabetes trials
summary: AstraZeneca’s GLP-1 pill showed promise in mid-stage obesity and diabetes studies, but it may still be too early to determine how it stacks up against other oral treatments.

title: City banks nearly $100M for next-gen RNAi drugs
summary: Co-founded by longtime Alnylam CEO John Maraganore, City has brought a clotting disorder drug into early human testing. An experimental therapy for Stargardt disease could soon follow.

title: Roche stakes $700M on Nurix’s protein-degrading drug
summary: Potentially worth up to $2.3 billion, the collaboration is a bet that drugs able to eliminate the “BTK” enzyme will prove superior to existing medicines that block its activity.

title: Tango data add to growing momentum for pancreatic cancer drugs
summary: A combination involving Tango’s experimental therapy appeared to boost response rates in a small clinical trial well beyond what would be expected, sparking a share surge.

title: Incyte to buy blood disorder drug in possibly $2B deal
summary: Acquiring Vega Therapeutics, an offshoot of "hub-and-spoke" biotech Star Therapeutics, would give Incyte a medicine that’s in late-stage testing and designed to control bleeding.

title: J&J to acquire Firefly, maker of ‘degrader’ antibody drugs, for $1B
summary: The buyout hands J&J a technology that merges elements of two popular drugmaking methods: protein degraders and antibody-drug conjugates.

title: ADA ‘26: Lilly’s dominance, Pfizer’s ‘foundational’ drug and Roche’s ‘me-too’ option
summary: At a big meeting for weight loss drugs, Lilly’s “triple G” therapy set a new standard as lingering doubts surrounded other competing treatments.

title: STAT+: UnitedHealthcare’s lactation billing feud, and fake strokes
summary: The biggest U.S. health insurer is changing how it pays for lactation counseling, and it could cut payment for many providers.

title: STAT+: Novo underwhelmed by drug it once fought Pfizer for
summary: Revolution and Tango yield promising cancer results, Incyte buys bleeding disorder drug, and more biotech news

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Lilly and Pfizer obesity drug data, Roche and J&J deals, and more
summary: Lilly disclosed new data that provide more details on the safety and tolerability of its next-generation obesity drug retatrutide

title: STAT+: J&J spends $1 billion to enter KRAS race for cancer treatments
summary: Johnson & Johnson said it will purchase the startup Firefly Bio for $1 billion in an effort to expand its work in the suddenly buzzy field of KRAS inhibitors.

title: Major readouts from the annual diabetes conference
summary: Why "Schedule F" matters, the Ebola outbreak response, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Combination of pancreatic cancer drugs from Tango, Revolution leads to high response rate
summary: A combination of pancreatic cancer drugs from Tango Therapeutics and Revolution Medicines led to a strong response rate in an early-stage trial.

title: Precision under pressure: Logistic solutions for nuclear medicine scalability
summary: Racing the clock: How precision logistics deliver life-saving nuclear medicine.

title: Innovation alone is no longer enough in biopharma
summary: In biopharma, the next edge may come from the systems that turn discovery into scale.

title: Opinion: $2 million gene therapy cures require a financing model
summary: “We do not lack cures. We lack the infrastructure to pay for and deliver them,” writes William Padula.

title: Opinion: American horses are obese, too
summary: “American horses are getting sick in ways that look remarkably familiar,” writes Joshua Moen.

title: When it’s time to save a limb, novel clinic meets unhoused people where they are
summary: Unmet vascular needs can lead to limb loss among unhoused patients. An unusual Massachusetts General Hospital program meets patients where they are.

summary: You're reading the web edition of STAT's ADA in 30 Seconds, from the American Diabetes Association's annual conference.

title: STAT+: New data may cast doubt on competitiveness of Boehringer’s obesity drug
summary: Boehringer Ingelheim’s obesity drug showed promise in cutting liver fat, but it was less impressive at overall weight loss, new data shows.

title: Study data convince Lundbeck to push new migraine drug forward
summary: A possible alternative to “anti-CGRP” medications, the drug didn’t meet some analysts’ expectations in mid-stage testing but still convinced Lundbeck to pursue further development.

title: Generic drugmakers gain key victory in ‘skinny label’ patent case
summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a knockoff version of Amarin’s Vascepa didn’t infringe on patents — a decision with far-reaching implications for the availability of generics.
